entities.name: śabda
entities.cite: the word itself is na purvo na parah — it has no earlier and later parts, because the meaning-bearer must be present whole wherever it is present at all

entities.name: nāda
entities.cite: Nada, realized sound, is krama-janman, sequence-born; that is simply what acoustic events are

entities.name: krama
entities.cite: the sequenceless is born *as if* divided, in the form of sequence

entities.name: brahman
entities.cite: as Brahman, undivided, appears as a world unfolding in time, so the word, undivided, appears as a string of phonemes

entities.name: jagat
entities.cite: as Brahman, undivided, appears as a world unfolding in time, so the word, undivided, appears as a string of phonemes

axioms.name: akrama
axioms.cite: the word itself is na purvo na parah — it has no earlier and later parts, because the meaning-bearer must be present whole wherever it is present at all

axioms.name: krama-janman
axioms.cite: Nada, realized sound, is krama-janman, sequence-born; that is simply what acoustic events are

axioms.name: vivartate
axioms.cite: akramah kramarupena bhedavan iva jayate, the sequenceless is born *as if* divided, in the form of sequence

axioms.name: vivartate
axioms.cite: as Brahman, undivided, appears as a world unfolding in time

denials.name: parinamate
denials.cite: the sequenceless is born *as if* divided, in the form of sequence

claims.name: krama-janman
claims.cite: Since audible sound is born in sequence

claims.name: akrama
claims.cite: the word is neither prior nor posterior

claims.name: vivartate
claims.cite: the sequenceless, taking on the form of sequence, is born as though divided

rejected_readings.label: real_transformation
rejected_readings.rendering: The word transforms into audible sound in sequence.
rejected_readings.why: Asserts parinamate (real transformation) where the verse uses vivarta (apparent division). The iva (as if) marks this as appearing divided without actual transformation.

rejected_readings.label: sequential_word
rejected_readings.rendering: The word is made up of sequential phonemes that together form meaning.
rejected_readings.why: Denies akrama by treating sabda as having temporal parts (earlier and later). The commentary insists the meaning-bearer must be present whole, not composed of sequential parts.

notes: The contract encodes the sphota doctrine's resolution of the phoneme paradox: sabda (the word) is akrama (whole, sequenceless) while nada (audible sound) is krama-janman (sequence-born). The vivarta relationship preserves sabda's integrity while explaining its manifestation as sequential sound. The brahman-jagat parallel establishes the structural homology between cosmology and linguistics.
